Title: Bikaverin as a molecular weapon: enhancing Fusarium oxysporum pathogenicity in bananas via rhizosphere microbiome manipulation

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. cubense Tropical Race 4
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial secondary metabolite that suppresses certain rhizosphere bacteria and fungi, indirectly enhancing pathogen colonization and banana wilt development
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FocBik6 is part of the conserved bikaverin biosynthetic gene cluster. 2. It is annotated as a bikaverin efflux pump. 3. Its metabolism-related role was not directly tested in this study.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as a cluster component but not a primary experimental subject in Results.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Amplicon sequencing of rhizosphere bacteria and fungi, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1197142
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Acidic pH; nitrogen source; microbial competition

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. cubense Tropical Race 4
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial metabolite that suppresses beneficial rhizosphere bacteria such as Bacillus and promotes a microbiome favorable for Fusarium colonization, thereby indirectly enhancing banana wilt pathogenicity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal mycelium/culture broth in Foc TR4; rhizosphere context during banana infection
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Typical type I fungal polyketide synthase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the initial formation of prebikaverin from precursor molecules in the bikaverin biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion abolishes bikaverin production and markedly reduces pathogenicity in nonsterile banana-soil conditions, but not under sterile/autoclaved conditions; bikaverin deficiency fails to suppress beneficial rhizosphere Bacillus and reduces Fusarium enrichment in the rhizosphere
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FocBik1 encodes the key PKS responsible for the initial and rate-limiting step of bikaverin biosynthesis, producing prebikaverin. 2. Knockout of FocBik1 eliminated detectable bikaverin production, confirming it is essential for bikaverin biosynthesis in Foc TR4. 3. ΔFocBik1 showed strongly reduced disease index in banana plantlets grown with natural microbiome influence, but no pathogenicity difference under sterile or autoclaved conditions, indicating an indirect microbiome-mediated virulence role. 4. FocBik1 expression and bikaverin production were induced by acidic pH, organic nitrogen context, and microbial competition. 5. Bikaverin produced via FocBik1 inhibited Bacillus velezensis, Actinomycetes strain DMS16689, and Botrytis cinerea, and reshaped rhizosphere bacterial/fungal communities.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene knockout; complementation; LC-MS metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR; banana pathogenicity assay; sterile/autoclaved condition assays; antimicrobial bioassays; rhizosphere 16S/ITS amplicon sequencing
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Acidic pH 4; yeast extract as nitrogen source versus NaNO3; microbial competition with Botrytis cinerea, Bacillus velezensis, and Actinomycetes strain DMS16689; sterile medium versus autoclaved soil versus natural rhizosphere conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced under acidic pH 4 with yeast extract and during dual culture with competing microbes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion slightly reduced conidiation and slightly increased sensitivity to sorbitol, H2O2, and Congo red; bikaverin not required for root infection or vegetative growth
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targeting FocBik1 or bikaverin biosynthesis could weaken microbiome-mediated virulence of Foc TR4; useful target for disease control and for screening/deploying bikaverin-resistant biocontrol bacteria such as Bacillus BR160
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA and ITS amplicon sequencing of rhizosphere microbiome, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1197142
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The metabolite effect is indirect on banana disease: bikaverin does not directly damage host tissues or enable root vascular infection under sterile conditions, but acts through rhizosphere microbiome manipulation.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. cubense Tropical Race 4
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial secondary metabolite indirectly associated with enhanced pathogenicity through rhizosphere microbiome manipulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putatively catalyzes an oxidative tailoring step in bikaverin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for biosynthetic conversion toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FocBik2 is part of the conserved bikaverin biosynthetic gene cluster. 2. It is annotated as a putative FAD-dependent monooxygenase. 3. Its direct contribution to bikaverin accumulation or pathogenicity was not experimentally tested in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Amplicon sequencing of rhizosphere bacteria and fungi, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1197142
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as cluster context; not a primary experimentally validated core gene in Results.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. cubense Tropical Race 4
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial secondary metabolite indirectly associated with enhanced pathogenicity through rhizosphere microbiome manipulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putatively catalyzes methylation step(s) in bikaverin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for biosynthetic conversion toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FocBik3 is part of the conserved bikaverin biosynthetic gene cluster. 2. It is annotated as an O-methyltransferase. 3. Its direct role was not experimentally tested in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Amplicon sequencing of rhizosphere bacteria and fungi, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1197142
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as cluster context; not a primary experimentally validated core gene in Results.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. cubense Tropical Race 4
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial metabolite indirectly promoting Foc TR4 pathogenicity through rhizosphere microbiome remodeling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation of bikaverin cluster expression (putative)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Putatively enhances expression of bikaverin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Cluster-associated regulator for bikaverin biosynthesis, but not directly tested here
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FocBik4 is located in the bikaverin biosynthetic cluster. 2. It is annotated as a transcription factor enhancer. 3. No direct functional validation was presented in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Amplicon sequencing of rhizosphere bacteria and fungi, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1197142
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cluster-context gene only; not a primary experimentally tested core gene in Results.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. cubense Tropical Race 4
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial metabolite indirectly promoting Foc TR4 pathogenicity through rhizosphere microbiome remodeling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation of bikaverin cluster expression (putative)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Putatively regulates expression of bikaverin cluster genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Cluster regulator of bikaverin pathway, but not directly functionally tested in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FocBik5 is part of the conserved bikaverin biosynthetic cluster. 2. It is annotated as a transcription factor regulating cluster expression. 3. Its direct effect on bikaverin accumulation or pathogenicity was not experimentally tested in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Amplicon sequencing of rhizosphere bacteria and fungi, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1197142
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cluster-context gene only; not a primary experimentally tested core gene in Results.
